title: Engineer field-relative undercut and traffic-window features, not just absolute tyre age
summary: F1 pit calls depend on whether a driver can undercut, avoid traffic, or react to rivals, not only on compound life.
rationale: F1 pit calls depend on whether a driver can undercut, avoid traffic, or react to rivals, not only on compound life. Formula 1 strategy coverage emphasizes undercut/overcut dynamics, pit windows, and opposite-of-rival calls. Your current best code uses absolute windows but little explicit opponent context.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year` + `LapNumber`, add percentiles/ranks for tyre life, lap delta, degradation, position, and pace loss; compute nearest-ahead/behind pit-risk summaries; add whether immediate rivals are in undercut range; optionally enrich with FastF1 `DriverAhead`/distance or same-lap field pit counts if available.
expected_effect: Should add signal for tactical reactions and explain why similar tyre states lead to different pit behavior depending on local traffic.
risk: Simple lap-group z-scores already underperformed in one weak baseline, so features must be strategy-aware and robust, not generic standardization noise.
